hypothesis: The actual future moving-fringe schedule determines a unique 2-adic zero survivor whose mismatch valuation exactly measures finite zero-streak length.
interpretation: The alternating inverse lift is exactly the shift-zero schedule survivor. Excluding period two is equivalent to proving its coupled output-pair transducer is not eventually all zero.
limitations: finite quotient agreement does not prove equality or inequality in the full 2-adic integers
the observed output-pair prefix does not prove infinitely many nonzero pairs
the result does not exclude eventual center period two
the result does not prove Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: The contraction, uniqueness, inverse-lift identification, mismatch valuation, and pair-transducer identities are proved in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_schedule_survivor.md. The executable campaign checks finite quotients and a bounded actual-path prefix.
question: problem1

exact_conclusions: every infinite future branch schedule has a unique 2-adic zero-survivor state
the actual alternating inverse lift is the shift-zero survivor and later survivors are its moving tails
the exact current zero-streak length is floor(v_2(x_m-X_m)/2)
the survivor binary digits are generated by the coupled G(11), G|_11 p q output-pair transducer
finite support is equivalent to that output-pair stream being eventually 00

schedule_survivor_pair_transducer.finite_scope_warning: Observed nonzero high pairs do not prove that the survivor has infinite ordinary support.

software.algorithm: exact 2-adic contractions, inverse-word sections, moving-fringe schedule, and output-pair transducer
